purpose: Catalog host and external capability options with source facts, validation commands, side-effect boundaries, and no-overclaim boundaries. Catalog membership is not runtime PASS.
no_overclaim_boundary: Catalog entry does not mean runtime PASS.
Catalog entry does not mean external capability available.
Governance packs are internal capability modules, not external plugins, skills, tools, MCP servers, browser tools, sub-agents, scripts, or fallbacks.
Do not claim automatic best-tool selection.
Do not claim universal plugin/skill/tool availability.
Do not claim official approval.
Do not claim marketplace approval.
Do not claim 1.0.0 production-ready.

source_facts: docs/requirements/codex-desktop-marketplace-e2e-0.45.0.md states Desktop marketplace-management E2E is still planned
capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Read-only manifest inspection only; no plugin install, enable, disable, upgrade, uninstall, network call, marketplace submission, or Desktop session mutation.
no_overclaim_boundary: Local plugin manifest presence is not Codex Desktop marketplace-management E2E PASS.
Catalog entry does not mean external capability available.
Do not claim marketplace approval.

capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Skill loading is instruction/context consumption; it does not install external packages, mutate browser state, or prove any external runtime capability.
no_overclaim_boundary: Skill entry availability is not universal host skill availability.
Governance packs are internal capability modules, not external capability availability.

capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Read-only local diagnostics; no writes, commits, pushes, network calls, package installs, browser actions, or external API calls.
no_overclaim_boundary: Diagnostic selection trace is not successful external execution.
Catalog entry does not mean runtime PASS.

source_facts: No repository fact proves a portable MCP server is installed or reachable for every host
skills/software-project-governance/infra/TOOLS.md indexes local workflow tools instead of MCP server manifests
capabilities.side_effect_boundary: No MCP calls are made by registry validation; future MCP use must declare server, auth, data access, network, and write boundaries.
no_overclaim_boundary: RESEARCH_ONLY MCP catalog entry is not runtime PASS.
Do not claim universal MCP availability.

source_facts: Host may expose browser control through enabled plugins, but this repository does not ship a browser runtime
docs/requirements/runtime-readiness-matrix-0.43.0.md separates runtime facts from universal support claims
capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Browser use may navigate pages and mutate browser/session state; registry validation does not open browsers or use logged-in sessions.
no_overclaim_boundary: Browser catalog entry is not proof a browser plugin is available in the current host.
Do not claim universal/full runtime support.

source_facts: agents/governance-developer.md defines the Governance Developer role
skills/software-project-governance/SKILL.md states unavailable true sub-agent separation must be recorded as degraded evidence
capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Sub-agent work may edit workflow product files only within assigned scope; degraded host execution must not be counted as independent review.
no_overclaim_boundary: Sub-agent catalog entry does not prove host-native sub-agent availability.
Developer self-review is not independent review.

capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Local validator execution reads repository files and may run local subprocess checks only when explicit subcommands request them; registry check itself performs no writes.
no_overclaim_boundary: Local script availability is not external tool availability.
Catalog entry does not mean runtime PASS.

source_facts: capability-context command can emit DEGRADED or BLOCKED traces from local files
docs/requirements/capability-discovery-orchestration-0.45.0.md requires restricted environments to output BLOCKED, DEGRADED, NOT_SUPPORTED, or NOT_FOUND when facts require it
capabilities.side_effect_boundary: Read-only fallback; no external execution, install, network, browser, commit, push, or API side effects.
no_overclaim_boundary: Fallback availability is not proof that preferred external capability executed.
Do not treat diagnostic selection trace as successful external execution.
